 Coelogyne and Sarcopodium are species of orchids, with Coelogyne being a large and diverse genus with over 200 species. The health benefits of Coelogyne and Sarcopodium include their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of these plants include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coelogyne and Sarcopodium include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Sarcopodium coelogyne is a genus of orchids. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae.
